    Mitchell is definitely lying. He claimed to be the Robber and then claimed to be the Troublemaker.

    James also changed his story. He started by implying he was the Seer, then later claimed he was the Robber.

    So Mitchell denied being the Troublemaker, then claimed to be the Robber, then claimed to be the Troublemaker.

    This is extremely suspicious. Mitchell is the most likely werewolf.

    Let's consider the Tanner. The Tanner wants to be voted out. Mitchell's erratic claims could be a Tanner strategy.

    James is *asking* if Mitchell said he trouble made people. Mitchell says "I did." But Mitchell *never* said he trouble made people before that point. He actually said the opposite ("I wasn't that").

    So Mitchell is not only contradicting himself, he's agreeing to a claim he never even made, and that he had previously denied. This is incredibly suspicious.

parsed_output.justification: Mitchell is the most inconsistent player in the game. He initially denied being the Troublemaker, then claimed to be the Robber who robbed Paul, and finally claimed to be the Troublemaker when James asked if he had already made that claim (despite never having done so). This pattern of contradictory claims and failure to maintain a consistent bluff strongly suggests he is a Werewolf.

validation.justification: Mitchell is the most inconsistent player in the game. He initially denied being the Troublemaker, then claimed to be the Robber who robbed Paul, and finally claimed to be the Troublemaker when James asked if he had already made that claim (despite never having done so). This pattern of contradictory claims and failure to maintain a consistent bluff strongly suggests he is a Werewolf.
